title: [CR] DodagState Python vs Rust: hysteresis comparison direction differs
description:
File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:156-158 vs rust/lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:231

Python:
    improvement = current_cost - best_cost
    if improvement < self.parent_switch_threshold:
        best, best_cost = current, current_cost

Rust:
    .filter(|&cur_cost| best_cost > cur_cost.saturating_sub(threshold))
    .map_or((best_addr, best_cost), |cur_cost| (cur_addr, cur_cost))

Both implement the same logic but expressed differently:
- Python: stick with current if improvement < threshold
- Rust: stick with current if best_cost > cur_cost - threshold

These are mathematically equivalent:
- Python: current_cost - best_cost < threshold => best_cost > current_cost - threshold
- Rust: best_cost > cur_cost.saturating_sub(threshold)

Wait - there's a subtle difference: saturating_sub vs regular subtraction. If threshold > cur_cost, Python would have negative improvement, Rust would have 0.

Example: cur_cost=100, threshold=150, best_cost=50
- Python: improvement = 100 - 50 = 50. 50 < 150? Yes, stick with current.
- Rust: cur_cost.saturating_sub(threshold) = 0. best_cost > 0? Yes (50 > 0), stick with current.

Same result. The saturating_sub handles potential underflow in Rust u16 vs Python unlimited int.

This is NOT a bug - the implementations are equivalent. Closing.
